trivia_bullets.text: Avacyn Restored (May 2012) concludes the Innistrad block, the three-set gothic-horror arc set on the plane of Innistrad. The set's narrative centers on the angel Avacyn's return and the restoration of humanity's hope against the plane's monsters. [Scryfall: set_name Avacyn Restored, released_at 2012-05-04]
Flavor text: "Finally, the principles of corpse animation applied to bloodless materials!" — Ludevic, necro-alchemist. [Scryfall]

Ludevic of Ulm is a reclusive necro-alchemist living in Havengul on Innistrad — the plane's most influential practitioner of the discipline, who mentored Geralf Cecani and created the flying skaab Kraum. His name first appeared on Ludevic's Test Subject // Ludevic's Abomination (original Innistrad block); demand for his own card led Wizards to print Ludevic, Necro-Alchemist in Commander 2016 (C16 no. 37). [MTG Wiki: Ludevic of Ulm, via WebSearch snippet; Scryfall: lore search ft:ludevic]
sources: MTG Wiki: Ludevic of Ulm, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Narstad Scrapper is the sole printing of this card — no reprint history. EDHREC rank 30,444 places it firmly in the long tail of format-unplayed commons; its primary use case is Pauper-legal colorless construct builds and bulk draft filler. [Scryfall: reprint: false, edhrec_rank: 30444; EDHREC route link confirmed via Scryfall]

Art is by Steven Belledin, a prolific MTG illustrator known for painting creatures with textural weight — corroded metal, organic decay, aged surfaces. The Narstad Scrapper aligns with his documented tendency toward grim-palette, material-surface-focused monster work. [Scryfall: artist field]
